commands.review_summary:
The PR moves OpenClaw image probing, resizing, encoding, and managed-image limit handling onto Rastermill, updates dependency pins and lockfiles, removes local fallback code/tests, and adjusts CI/script follow-ups.

PR surface: Source -1041, Tests -662, Docs 0, Config -1, Other -7. Total -1711 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user-reported issue, but the PR blockers are source-reproducible from the current-main and PR-head code paths. The strongest checks are the dependency pin, removed backend option path, missing EXIF unavailable catch, and managed-image limit mismatch.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers require maintainer release, supply-chain, compatibility, and dirty-merge decisions rather than a safe single automated repair loop.

target.title: Refactor image processing onto Rastermill
target.body:
## Summary

- Delegate OpenClaw image resize/encode/probe mechanics to Rastermill and remove local image parser/codec fallback code.
- Update OpenClaw to pushed Rastermill commit `91741e538f3073c9cb1bb70424ce617eae5b15e2`, including the unified `encode(input, options?)` API for exact formats, byte budgets, auto format selection, dimension limits, metadata policy, and small-limit byte-budget downscaling.
- Keep OpenClaw policy in OpenClaw: media caps, managed attachment warnings, SDK compatibility names, and orientation-aware managed attachment limits.
- Fix PR CI follow-ups already on this branch: satisfy the Opengrep `git add --` guard and run the Windows node lane on Node 22.19.0, matching the supported floor and current Blacksmith Windows image.

## Verification

- Rastermill: `pnpm check`
- Rastermill: `pnpm test` (2 files / 57 tests)
- Rastermill: `pnpm build`
- Rastermill: `git diff --check`
- Rastermill: `autoreview --mode local` clean after fixing accepted findings.
- OpenClaw: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/image-ops.rastermill-adapter.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism` (7 files / 152 tests)
- OpenClaw: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- OpenClaw: `git diff --check`
- OpenClaw CI: current-head metadata checks passed; full branch checks are expected to rerun after the dependency repin.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Image processing mechanics now live in Rastermill instead of OpenClaw-local parser/codec/fallback stacks.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/OpenClaw repo wrappers, Rastermill commit `91741e538f3073c9cb1bb70424ce617eae5b15e2` pushed to `openclaw/rastermill`, and OpenClaw branch head `62b1676aa964c61369e1545f0ccaa081257c1a0a`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest media/browser/agent/managed-image tests, full project typecheck, Rastermill test/check/build, and Codex autoreview on Rastermill.
Evidence after fix: Rastermill passed 2 files / 57 tests; OpenClaw focused media/browser/agent/managed-image set passed 7 files / 152 tests; OpenClaw project typecheck passed; Rastermill autoreview ended clean.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw call sites now use Rastermill's unified `encode` API for PNG byte-budget search, web media auto optimization, and managed image dimension limits while keeping OpenClaw-specific policy at the call sites.
What was not tested: no live HEIC conversion fixture against installed native tools in this PR run; latest local `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by unrelated AWS shrinkwrap-regeneration drift in the working checkout, so the PR relies on focused local proof plus GitHub checks for the broad gate.
target.author: steipete
